meta.notes: Read-only research log. No trades are ever placed by this routine.

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: 78 of 80 required events reported as of company's May 11, 2026 update; final analysis triggers on 80th event; topline readout guided for 2H 2026
catalyst.next_check: watch for 8-K / press release announcing 80th event

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 69.8% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within cap but second-largest single-name risk; lockup supply event ~2-3 weeks out

flags: SLS at 69.8% of NAV is nearly double the 35% single-name trim threshold, in a pre-binary-catalyst biotech with no news that breaks the thesis but no catalyst confirmation either.
Zero USD cash buffer - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).
SPCX facing its first staggered lockup unlock in ~2-3 weeks; stock already down 4-5% pre-market with no negative credit news (bond was oversubscribed, investment-grade).

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: Still 78 of 80 required events as of last confirmed update; no 8-K or press release announcing the 80th event found as of 2026-07-17. Only unrelated 8-Ks filed in July (executive employment/severance agreements). Stock has been highly volatile on WSB/social-media buzz (spiked ~18-25% intraday on multiple days, then given back) layered on top of the pending binary catalyst, unrelated to trial data.
catalyst.next_check: watch for 8-K / press release announcing 80th event; topline still guided for 2H 2026

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 71.4% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold, worse than last run's 69.8%

catalyst.name: $25B investment-grade bond deal (closed) + staggered IPO lockup + Q2 earnings
catalyst.status: NEW since last run: a failed Starship V3 launch test attempt pushed shares below the $135 IPO price for the first time. Q2 2026 earnings date confirmed for Aug 6, 2026; first lockup tranche (20% of the 4.6B-share 180-day block) unlocks the 2nd trading day after earnings (~Aug 10, 2026). Further 7% tranches Aug-Oct; a larger 28% tranche after Q3 earnings (~Nov); full lockup expiry Dec 8, 2026. Bond thesis unchanged (oversubscribed, investment-grade, retired the bridge loan).
catalyst.next_check: Aug 6 2026 Q2 earnings print; stock reaction into the ~Aug 10 first lockup tranche

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within 35% cap but second-largest single-name risk; confirmed lockup/earnings event now ~3 weeks out and fresh negative operational news (launch failure) has compounded the pre-existing lockup overhang

flags: NAV down 9.75% since last run (EUR 1107.73 -> 999.66) over 6 days, driven by broad declines in both positions; no new capital added.

SLS at 71.4% of NAV is now more than double the 35% single-name trim threshold and has worsened since last run; no trial-thesis-breaking news found, but the position's risk concentration is objectively worse.

SPCX hit fresh negative news (failed Starship V3 launch test, first close below IPO price) on top of the previously flagged lockup overhang; Q2 earnings (Aug 6) and first lockup tranche (~Aug 10) are now confirmed and ~3 weeks out.
Zero USD cash buffer persists - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).

Broader tape is risk-off: oil sharply higher and chip stocks pressured on renewed US-Iran/Gulf tensions, plus new 25% US tariffs on Brazil - a headwind backdrop, not specific to either position but relevant to today's gap-down in SPCX.
